questions.question: What is workplace digital wellness?

options.text: Banning all technology from the workplace
Strategies and norms to manage technology use at work in ways that preserve focus, prevent burnout, and support wellbeing

questions.explanation: Workplace digital wellness refers to practices, norms, and boundaries around technology use at work — managing email, meetings, notifications, and communication tools to protect focus, prevent burnout, and maintain wellbeing.

questions.question: What is 'email apnea' and why does it matter?

options.text: The tendency to hold your breath or breathe shallowly while checking email, which increases stress and tension

questions.explanation: 'Email apnea' describes the unconscious breath-holding or shallow breathing many people do while reading email, which activates the stress response. It's a physical manifestation of email-related anxiety.

questions.question: What is an effective workplace strategy for managing email overload?

options.text: Check email continuously throughout the day to stay on top of everything
Batch process email at designated times (e.g., 9am, 1pm, 4pm), turn off notifications, and use filters/rules to prioritize
Never check email and let it pile up
Respond to every email immediately as it arrives

questions.explanation: Batch processing (checking email at specific times, not continuously) reduces attention fragmentation. Turning off email notifications and using filters helps prioritize important messages without constant interruption.

questions.question: What is the 'right to disconnect' and why does it matter?

options.text: A law requiring all employees to work remotely
The right to disconnect from work communication (email, messages) outside of work hours to prevent burnout and protect work-life boundaries
The ability to turn off your computer
A policy banning all technology use

questions.explanation: The 'right to disconnect' (law in some countries, policy in some companies) protects employees from being expected to respond to work communication outside of work hours. It prevents 'always-on' culture and supports recovery and boundaries.

questions.question: Reflect on your workplace technology use. Do you feel pressure to respond to emails/messages outside work hours? Do you have clear boundaries between 'work time' and 'personal time'? What's one boundary you could set to improve your workplace digital wellness?
